name: J1939TestChkCreate_SignalRangeViolation
title: J1939TestChkCreate_SignalRangeViolation
syntax: long J1939TestChkCreate_SignalRangeViolation( dword sourceAddr, dword destAddr, dbMsg msg, dbSignal signal, float minSignalValue, float maxSignalValue, char callback[] )
description: Checks the value of a signal. The given interval (inclusive the limits) should be never left. An event will be generated, if the physical signal value lies outside the given value interval.

parameters.name: sourceAddr
parameters.description: source address of the PG or Null Address (0xFE) for wildcard

parameters.name: destAddr
parameters.description: destination address of the PG or Null Address (0xFE) for wildcard, ignored for broadcast PGs

parameters.name: signal
parameters.description: signal from database, which should be observed

parameters.name: minSignalValue
parameters.description: minimum limit of the signal range, 0 < x < maxSignalValue

parameters.name: maxSignalValue
parameters.description: maximum limit of the signal range, minSignalValue < x < MAX_FLOAT

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: name of the callback, which is called when the check fails or 0, signature: void Callback( long checked )

return: source address of the PG or Null
availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
